config.name: PairCorrectly
config.description: Step 12.0 baseline — matched-pair round-robin. Every archetype paired with its designed bot: bulwark-control+fortress, ember-aggro+hellfire, hollow-disruption+reaper, tide-thorn-combo+wavebreaker. Cross-pair only (no mirror) so cells are about matched-pair-A vs matched-pair-B, not self-variance. logLevel=llm so the JSONL learning log lands under logs/tournaments/.

notes: Clear winner: 'TiThWave' leads runner-up by 78 percentage points.
3 matchup(s) drew ≥ 40% of games — worth inspecting for stall states.

learningLog: Tournament 'PairCorrectly' ran 240 games across 6 matchup(s). Pairs: BulFort=(deck:bulwark-control,bot:experimental/2026-04-19-fortress), EmbHell=(deck:ember-aggro,bot:experimental/2026-04-19-hellfire), HolReap=(deck:hollow-disruption,bot:experimental/2026-04-19-reaper), TiThWave=(deck:tide-thorn-combo,bot:experimental/2026-04-19-wavebreaker)

Matchup BulFort vs EmbHell: deck 'bulwark-control' piloted by 'experimental/2026-04-19-fortress' versus deck 'ember-aggro' piloted by 'experimental/2026-04-19-hellfire'. BulFort won 2/40 (BWins:1,Draws:37, 5%). Different deck + bot, close outcome — either both pairs are balanced or the dominant factor cancels out. Follow up with a controlled matchup. Average game length: 404 steps.

Matchup BulFort vs HolReap: deck 'bulwark-control' piloted by 'experimental/2026-04-19-fortress' versus deck 'hollow-disruption' piloted by 'experimental/2026-04-19-reaper'. BulFort won 17/40 (BWins:1,Draws:22, 42%). Different deck + bot: the (experimental/2026-04-19-fortress, bulwark-control) pair wins — hard to attribute to either axis alone; follow up with a controlled same-bot or same-deck matchup to isolate. Average game length: 276 steps.

Matchup BulFort vs TiThWave: deck 'bulwark-control' piloted by 'experimental/2026-04-19-fortress' versus deck 'tide-thorn-combo' piloted by 'experimental/2026-04-19-wavebreaker'. TiThWave won 37/40 (AWins:3,Draws:0, A side 8%). Different deck + bot: the (experimental/2026-04-19-wavebreaker, tide-thorn-combo) pair wins — hard to attribute to either axis alone; follow up with a controlled same-bot or same-deck matchup to isolate. Average game length: 351 steps.

Matchup EmbHell vs HolReap: deck 'ember-aggro' piloted by 'experimental/2026-04-19-hellfire' versus deck 'hollow-disruption' piloted by 'experimental/2026-04-19-reaper'. EmbHell won 10/40 (BWins:0,Draws:30, 25%). Different deck + bot: the (experimental/2026-04-19-hellfire, ember-aggro) pair wins — hard to attribute to either axis alone; follow up with a controlled same-bot or same-deck matchup to isolate. Average game length: 355 steps.

Matchup EmbHell vs TiThWave: deck 'ember-aggro' piloted by 'experimental/2026-04-19-hellfire' versus deck 'tide-thorn-combo' piloted by 'experimental/2026-04-19-wavebreaker'. TiThWave won 39/40 (AWins:1,Draws:0, A side 2%). Different deck + bot: the (experimental/2026-04-19-wavebreaker, tide-thorn-combo) pair wins — hard to attribute to either axis alone; follow up with a controlled same-bot or same-deck matchup to isolate. Average game length: 373 steps.

Matchup HolReap vs TiThWave: deck 'hollow-disruption' piloted by 'experimental/2026-04-19-reaper' versus deck 'tide-thorn-combo' piloted by 'experimental/2026-04-19-wavebreaker'. TiThWave won 39/40 (AWins:1,Draws:0, A side 2%). Different deck + bot: the (experimental/2026-04-19-wavebreaker, tide-thorn-combo) pair wins — hard to attribute to either axis alone; follow up with a controlled same-bot or same-deck matchup to isolate. Average game length: 249 steps.

Across all matchups, 'TiThWave' (deck:tide-thorn-combo,bot:experimental/2026-04-19-wavebreaker) outperformed 'HolReap' (deck:hollow-disruption,bot:experimental/2026-04-19-reaper). Pattern: the top pair's bot profile is worth studying as a candidate to pair with similar decks; the weakest pair suggests either a weight-table mismatch or a deck that disagrees with the bot's scoring priorities.
Observation: Clear winner: 'TiThWave' leads runner-up by 78 percentage points.
Observation: 3 matchup(s) drew ≥ 40% of games — worth inspecting for stall states.

LEARNING HINT: When choosing a bot for a new deck, prefer profiles whose matched decks in this tournament share archetype tags (aggro/control/midrange) with the new deck. Avoid pairing a utility bot tuned for defence with an aggro deck — it will underweight tempo plays.
